title: BenchmarkCitation
description: One (benchmark x citing document) record. Topline entity is the BENCHMARK. ALL benchmarks are tracked regardless of Harbor status; on_harbor is just a flag.

benchmark_canonical.description: Normalized via aliases.yaml. null if unmatched (=> needs_review true).

benchmark_raw.description: Verbatim benchmark name as written in the doc.

type.description: Within-document prominence. Headline (blog hero) weighs 3x; table_row is discounted by table_total so a row in a 50-benchmark table counts far less.

table_total.description: Number of benchmarks in the doc; used to discount deep table rows.

reported.description: Only fill fields actually stated in the doc. Never infer.

type.description: HOW the benchmark is run: agentic (tools / environment / multi-step) vs chat (one-shot Q&A / exam / non-agentic). Harbor prefers agentic. Kept ORTHOGONAL to domain. Surfaced separately, never folded into the priority score. null if benchmark unresolved.

domain.description: Subject matter of the benchmark. Do NOT conflate with type (a coding benchmark can be chat or agentic). null if unresolved.

on_harbor.description: true iff this benchmark is already a confirmed Harbor (name,version) entry.

harbor_name.description: matched Harbor entry name, if any.

score_pending.description: true = benchmark cited but numeric score not yet extracted from primary (tracked, not a human-decision).

needs_review.description: true = genuine HUMAN decision needed (unmatched name / Harbor collision / config-dependent / chart-only). Surfaced as a GitHub issue.

review_issue.description: GitHub issue # (in benchmark-citations) tracking this flag. Issues are the source of truth for the review queue.
